title: 3 Reasons SoundHound AI Stock Could Keep Climbing
author: Micah Zimmerman
description: SoundHound AI is transitioning from voice-recognition software to agentic AI that can complete real-world tasks like ordering food and booking reservations. The company is diversifying across automotive, restaurant, retail, and customer service sectors, and recently secured an expanded partnership with Casey's General Stores. However, the stock remains speculative with high valuation risks, unprofitability, and competition from tech giants like Amazon and Apple.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: The article highlights three bullish developments: shift to agentic AI with transaction participation, diversification across multiple industries reducing single-customer risk, and customer expansion signals like Casey's General Stores renewal. These represent real business progress and revenue model improvement.

title: BigBear.ai vs. SoundHound AI: Which AI Stock Is a Better Buy in 2026?
author: John Ballard
description: The article compares two AI stocks with divergent strategies: BigBear.ai focuses on government defense contracts with declining revenue and significant legal risks, while SoundHound AI pursues rapid commercial expansion in automotive and restaurant sectors with strong revenue growth. Despite both companies being unprofitable, SoundHound AI is recommended as the better long-term buy due to its expanding customer base and clearer path to profitability through scale.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Declining revenue (down 19.3% YoY to $127.7M), significant net losses ($293.9M), material class action lawsuit, financial restatement requirements, high customer concentration risk (51% from customers >10% of revenue), and narrow market focus in government contracts with no clear growth trajectory.

title: SoundHound AI Stock Is Down 70% From Its 52-Week High. Time to Buy?
author: Will Healy
description: SoundHound AI stock has plummeted 70% from its $21 peak in October 2025, despite strong revenue growth. While the P/S ratio of 14 is more attractive than previous peaks exceeding 100, the company faces significant headwinds: it has never been profitable, continues to dilute shareholders (68% share increase since early 2024), and revenue growth is decelerating from 52% to projected 38% this year. Analysts suggest the stock is more likely to stagnate or fall further than recover.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite attractive current valuation metrics, the company faces multiple red flags: persistent operating losses ($23M in Q1 2026), significant shareholder dilution (68% increase since early 2024), and decelerating revenue growth (52% to projected 38% then 18%). The article concludes the stock is more likely to stagnate or decline further rather than recover, making it unsuitable as a buy at current levels.

title: Down 72%, Should You Buy the Dip in SoundHound AI?
author: John Ballard
description: SoundHound AI stock has plunged 72% from all-time highs despite rapid business expansion with 52% YoY revenue growth. The company's voice AI technology is gaining traction with restaurants and automakers, and its new OASYS agentic AI platform has helped a Fortune 100 company save $10 million quarterly. However, the company remains unprofitable with a $26 million adjusted net loss and faces competition from tech giants like Google. The voice AI market is projected to reach $47 billion by 2034, offering significant long-term potential for patient investors willing to tolerate volatility.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Despite significant stock decline and current unprofitability, the company shows strong revenue growth (52% YoY), successful real-world applications generating measurable value ($10M quarterly savings), and operates in a high-growth market projected to reach $47 billion by 2034. The pullback is presented as a potential buying opportunity for long-term investors.

title: Joby Aviation Stock Sinks on Latest News. Will the eVTOL Ever Recover Its Lost Value?
author: John Bromels
description: Joby Aviation's stock fell 20% last week following a stronger-than-expected jobs report, which raised concerns about potential Federal Reserve interest rate hikes. Despite positive company-specific news including NYC eVTOL demonstration flights, the macroeconomic outlook for high-growth companies dimmed. The stock is down over 50% from its all-time high, though FAA approval could trigger a significant recovery.

insights.sentiment_reasoning: Stock fell 20% last week due to macroeconomic concerns about interest rate hikes. Despite positive company-specific news (NYC demonstration flights, lawsuit dismissal), the broader economic outlook for high-growth companies weakened. Stock is down 50% from all-time high and remains speculative and volatile.
